ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM Exchange post-exploit: w3wp.exe spawning shells & dropping .aspx web shells (ProxyLogon chain)
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical HAFNIUM tradecraft from the 2021 Operation Exchange Marauder zero-day chain (CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065): the IIS Exchange worker process w3wp.exe spawning cmd/powershell/csc/net/whoami, or writing .aspx web shells (China Chopper / ASPXSpy variants) into Exchange virtual directories such as \FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\, \aspnet_client\system_web\ and \Microsoft\Exchange Server*\ClientAccess\. This pattern is repeatedly attributed to Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) by Microsoft / CrowdStrike / Volexity and is rare on a properly patched estate.
ucs.splunk: (| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_name) as child_proc values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe","csc.exe","cvtres.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","schtasks.exe","procdump.exe","rundll32.exe") by host Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process_id | rename Processes.* as * | eval signal="w3wp_child") | append [| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as path values(Filesystem.process_name) as proc from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name="w3wp.exe" Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" (Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\owa\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\aspnet_client\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ClientAccess\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server*\\V15\\*") by host Filesystem.file_name | rename Filesystem.* as * | eval signal="aspx_dropped_by_w3wp"] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 14d;
let exch_paths = dynamic([@"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\", @"\aspnet_client\", @"\ClientAccess\", @"\inetpub\wwwroot\", @"\Exchange Server\"]);
let proc_signal = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MSExchange","OWAAppPool","ECPAppPool","RpcProxy")
         or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has @"\Exchange Server\"
    | where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe",
                          "whoami.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe",
                          "csc.exe","cvtres.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe",
                          "schtasks.exe","procdump.exe","rundll32.exe","hostname.exe")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, Signal="w3wp_child";
let file_signal = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".asmx"
    | where FolderPath has_any (exch_paths)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
              InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, Signal="aspx_dropped_by_w3wp",
              AccountName=InitiatingProcessAccountName, ProcessCommandLine=InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
union proc_signal, file_signal
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let lookback = 14d;
let exch_paths = dynamic([@"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\", @"\aspnet_client\", @"\ClientAccess\", @"\inetpub\wwwroot\", @"\Exchange Server\"]);
let proc_signal = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
    | where NewProcessName endswith_any ("\\cmd.exe","\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe","\\net.exe",
        "\\net1.exe","\\whoami.exe","\\ipconfig.exe","\\nltest.exe","\\systeminfo.exe",
        "\\csc.exe","\\cvtres.exe","\\certutil.exe","\\bitsadmin.exe","\\schtasks.exe",
        "\\procdump.exe","\\rundll32.exe","\\hostname.exe")
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
              ParentProcessName, Signal="w3wp_child";
let file_signal = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where ActingProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
    | where TargetFileName endswith_any (".aspx",".ashx",".asmx")
    | where TargetFilePath has_any (exch_paths)
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer=DvcHostname, Account=ActorUsername,
              NewProcessName=TargetFilePath, CommandLine=ActingProcessCommandLine,
              ParentProcessName=ActingProcessName, Signal="aspx_dropped_by_w3wp";
union proc_signal, file_signal
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: HAFNIUM / Silk Typhoon Exchange w3wp.exe Spawns Recon or Shell Process
id: 6e74e0b1-31b2-4b0e-9a55-silktyphoon01
status: stable
description: Detects the Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) ProxyLogon post-exploitation pattern where the IIS Exchange application pool worker w3wp.exe spawns interpreters or recon LOLBins. Signed off by MSRC/CISA after CVE-2021-26855 chain.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/06
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.g0125
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\csc.exe'
      - '\cvtres.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\schtasks.exe'
      - '\procdump.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
  condition: parent and child
falsepositives:
  - Exchange cumulative-update installs (rare, scheduled, not interactive)
  - Legitimate management agents that exec under the Exchange app pool identity
level: high
ucs.rationale: This combination — w3wp.exe-as-parent of an interpreter/LOLBin AND/OR w3wp.exe writing .aspx into the Exchange OWA auth path — is the signature of HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon's ProxyLogon chain (Microsoft, CISA AA21-062A, Volexity). Random APTs that exploit Exchange use other web-shell paths or different droppers; this exact tuple of (Exchange virtual dir + w3wp drop + native recon LOLBin) was the public
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon cloud pivot: credential added to existing OAuth app / service principal for tenant persistence (T1098.001)
ucs.description: Hunts Silk Typhoon's 2024-2025 tradecraft (per Microsoft Threat Intel, March 2025): after compromising an MSP, IT vendor, or privileged access management tool, the actor abuses stolen admin credentials to add a new password/certificate (KeyCredential / PasswordCredential) to a multi-tenant OAuth application or existing service principal — granting persistent, hard-to-spot programmatic access to mailboxes (Mail.Read), files, and Entra ID. Fires when a credential is added to a service principal whose secret is then used to sign in from an unfamiliar IP within 24h.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Change.object) as sp_object values(Change.user) as actor values(Change.src) as actor_ip from datamodel=Change where (Change.action="modified" OR Change.action="created") (Change.object_category="service_principal" OR Change.object_category="application") (Change.change_type="AAD" OR sourcetype="azure:aad:audit") (Change.command="Add service principal credentials" OR Change.command="Update application*Certificates and secrets management" OR Change.command="Add owner to service principal") by Change.object_id Change.object | rename Change.* as * | join type=inner object_id [| tstats summariesonly=t count as signin_count values(Authentication.src) as signin_ips values(Authentication.app) as app from datamodel=Authentication where (Authentication.signature="ServicePrincipalSignInLogs" OR sourcetype="azure:aad:serviceprincipal") Authentication.action="success" earliest=-24h by Authentication.user_id | rename Authentication.user_id as object_id Authentication.* as *] | where signin_count>0 AND NOT match(signin_ips, "^(10\.|172\.(1[6-9]|2[0-9]|3[01])\.|192\.168\.)") | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | table firstTime lastTime object actor actor_ip signin_ips app signin_count
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 14d;
let sp_cred_adds = CloudAppEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where Application == "Office 365" or ApplicationId == "AzureActiveDirectory"
    | where ActionType in~ ("Add service principal credentials.","Update application – Certificates and secrets management","Update service principal","Add owner to service principal","Update application")
    | extend SpId = tostring(parse_json(tostring(RawEventData)).Target[0].ID)
    | extend SpName = tostring(parse_json(tostring(RawEventData)).Target[0].Name)
    | extend Actor = AccountDisplayName
    | project CredAddTime=Timestamp, Actor, ActorIP=IPAddress, ActorCountry=CountryCode,
              SpId, SpName, ActionType, IsAdminOperation;
let sp_signins = AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where TokenIssuerType == "AzureAD"
    | where AccountDisplayName has_any ("ServicePrincipal","")
        or isnotempty(ApplicationId)
    | where ErrorCode == 0
    | project SignInTime=Timestamp, ApplicationId, AppDisplayName, IPAddress, Country, City, ResourceDisplayName;
sp_cred_adds
| join kind=inner sp_signins on $left.SpId == $right.ApplicationId
| where SignInTime between (CredAddTime .. CredAddTime + 24h)
| where Country !in~ ("GB","US")  // tune to org's expected countries
      or ActorIP != IPAddress
| project CredAddTime, SignInTime,
          MinutesAfter = datetime_diff('minute', SignInTime, CredAddTime),
          SpName, SpId, ActionType, Actor, ActorIP, ActorCountry,
          SigninIP=IPAddress, SigninCountry=Country, ResourceDisplayName, AppDisplayName
| order by SignInTime asc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let lookback = 14d;
let sp_cred_adds = AuditLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
    | where LoggedByService in ("Core Directory","Application Proxy")
    | where OperationName in~ ("Add service principal credentials","Update application – Certificates and secrets management","Update service principal","Update application","Add owner to service principal")
    | where Result == "success"
    | extend Actor = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.userPrincipalName),
             ActorIP = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.ipAddress),
             AppActor = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).app.displayName)
    | mv-expand TargetResources
    | extend SpName = tostring(TargetResources.displayName),
             SpId   = tostring(TargetResources.id),
             AppId  = tostring(TargetResources.userPrincipalName)
    | extend ModifiedProps = tostring(TargetResources.modifiedProperties)
    | where ModifiedProps has_any ("KeyCredential","PasswordCredential","AppAddKey")
    | project CredAddTime=TimeGenerated, OperationName, Actor, AppActor, ActorIP, SpName, SpId;
let sp_signins = AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
    | where ResultType == 0
    | project SignInTime=TimeGenerated, AppId, ServicePrincipalName,
              IPAddress, Location, ResourceDisplayName;
sp_cred_adds
| join kind=inner sp_signins on $left.SpId == $right.AppId
| where SignInTime between (CredAddTime .. CredAddTime + 24h)
| where IPAddress != ActorIP
| project CredAddTime, SignInTime,
          MinutesAfter = datetime_diff('minute', SignInTime, CredAddTime),
          SpName, SpId, OperationName, Actor, AppActor, ActorIP,
          SignInIP=IPAddress, SignInLocation=Location, ResourceDisplayName
| order by SignInTime asc
ucs.rationale: Microsoft's March 2025 Silk Typhoon advisory specifically called out this technique: post-compromise of a downstream IT/MSP/PAM provider, the actor adds new password/certificate credentials to an existing multi-tenant OAuth application or service principal — turning that SP into a long-lived backdoor that survives password resets and bypasses MFA.
